scope: Defines the safe successor candidate for IF09-FIND-001 S3 track after PURG-01.1 classification.
successor_candidate_name: S3 Track Scope and Remediation Planning Readiness
minimum_requirements_for_future_purg02: Confirm that the roadmap-defined PURG-02 remains Reproduction / RED Baseline and is not renamed or auto-opened by this candidate artifact.
Materialize an S3 scope statement for IF09-FIND-001 that stays within medium-severity hardening boundaries and the verified active_context_governance affected layer.
Preserve the source packet, IF10 graph, and handoff manifest hashes as the authoritative evidence chain for any later RED-baseline preparation.
State explicitly which reproduction, replay, and mutation units will be used if a future operator-approved roadmap-defined PURG-02 is opened.
Record the blocked condition if the future RED baseline cannot prove the finding without remediation or if any required fixture/oracle becomes ambiguous.
Keep remediation apply, runtime mutation, real execution, secrets, product, and Bedrock authorization fully false.
roadmap_alignment_notes: The Purgatorium roadmap defines PURG-S0..PURG-S3 as conditional tracks by finding, derived from severity and affected layer.
The roadmap defines PURG-02 as Reproduction / RED Baseline.
The roadmap defines PURG-03 as Remediation Plan Compiler.
This successor candidate therefore records readiness and scope only; it does not authorize or rename any roadmap-defined later phase.
